LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER WILLIAM HUME'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT WILL NOT EXECUTE PLAINTIFF'S FORM OF JUDGMENT. THE DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL. THE JUDGMENT SHALL READ: "THE ACTION IS DISMISSED BASED UPON THE ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER ENTERED ON JANUARY 7, 2009, AND BECAUSE THE PARTIES CONCEDE THAT ALL REMAINING CLAIMS IN THE CASE ARE MOOT." ORDER & JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOHN STEWART; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185

LAW AND MOTION 301, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING RECONSIDERATION OF DEMURRER IN LIGHT OF CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT CASE (SANDERS)- RESPONDENTS STATE BAR DEMURRER TO VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE OR ON THE ALTERNATIVE, COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF- THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AND PART OF THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION TO THE EXTENT THAT THE PETITIONER IS REQUESTING INFORMATION REGARDING GRADING POLICY. THE COURT HAS NO JURISDICTION. THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS PARTLY OVERRULED AS TO GENERAL INFORMATION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: MARY ELLEN SCHERER CSR # 5208

REPLY BRIEF FILED BY RESPONDENT THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, A PUBLIC CORPORATION BARBIERI, DEAN AN INDIVIDUAL MURPHY, GAYLE AN INDIVIDUAL
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-04-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : RESPONDENTS THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, A PUBLIC CORPORATION, DEAN BARBIERI AND GAYLE MURPHY'S DEMURRER TO VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DECISIONS AND POLICIES CONCERNING WHAT INFORMATION TO RELEASE REGARDING BAR EXAM GRADING AND RESULTS IS PART AND PARCEL OF THE ADMISSIONS PROCESS CARRIED OUT BY THE STATE BAR IN ITS ROLE AS ADJUNCT TO THE SUPREME COURT'S SUPERVISION OF ADMISSION TO PRACTICE LAW. THEREFORE, THIS CASE FALLS WITHIN THE SUPREME COURT'S INHERENT AUTHORITY AND ITS ORGINAL EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION. MOREOVER, THIS COURT COULD NOT ORDER THE SUPREME COURT'S ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT TO RELEASE INFORMATION CONCERNING THE ADMISSIONS PROCESS IT HAD DECIDED NOT TO RELEASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Equitable Relief From Order Sustaining General Demurrer
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF WILLIAM HUME's Motion For Equitable Relief From Order Sustaining General Demurrer
is Denied. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate an "extrinsic mistake" occurred necessitating equitable relief. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn And Mtn For Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-01-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL VON LOEWENFELDT OF KERR & WAGSTAFFE, TEL. NO. 357-8909 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER WILLIAM HUME's Motion for Judgment is granted in part. The court will not execute plaintiff's form of judgment. The defendant shall prepare a judgment of dismissal. The judgment shall read: "The action is dismissed based upon the order sustaining defendant's demurrer entered on January 7, 2009, and because the parties concede that all remaining claims in the case are moot." ORDER & JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =(302/JKS)

Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn And Mtn For Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-16-2014 Continued To Aug-01-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER WILLIAM HUME'S Motion For Judgment is continued to August 1, 2014, to give the moving party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)

Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn And Mtn For Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-20-2014 Continued To Jul-16-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Feb-20-2014 Advanced From Mar-18-14 To Mar-11-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Timothy L. McCandless, Esq. of the Law Offices of Timothy L. McCandless, Tel. 951.356.1303, limited scope attorney for plaintiff, via Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: Mr. McCandless did not give notice that he will be appearing to contest the tentative ruling. The Court did not hear arguments. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. THOMAS R. BURKE, ESQ. and SANJAY M. NANGIA, ESQ.'s MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PETITIONER WILLIAM SNOW HUME is granted. Counsel presents good cause for withdrawal and Mr. Hume is not prejudiced. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Aug-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. DUFFY CAROLAN OF DAVIS WRIGHT TREMAINE LLP, TEL. NO. 276-6585 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. MICAHEL VON LOEWENFELDT OF KERR & WAGSTAFFE, TEL. NO. 371-8500 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT AND MOVING PARTY.
ATTY. RACHEL SIMONE GRUNBERG, TEL. NO. 538-2309 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT AND MOVING PARTY THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : RESPONDENTS THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, A PUBLIC CORPORATION, DEAN BARBIERI AND GAYLE MURPHY'S DEMURRER TO VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. DECISIONS AND POLICIES CONCERNING WHAT INFORMATION TO RELEASE REGARDING BAR EXAM GRADING AND RESULTS IS PART AND PARCEL OF THE ADMISSIONS PROCESS CARRIED OUT BY THE STATE BAR IN ITS ROLE AS ADJUNCT TO THE SUPREME COURT'S SUPERVISION OF ADMISSION TO PRACTICE LAW. THEREFORE, THIS CASE FALLS WITHIN THE SUPREME COURT'S INHERENT AUTHORITY AND ITS ORGINAL EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION. MOREOVER, THIS COURT COULD NOT ORDER THE SUPREME COURT'S ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT TO RELEASE INFORMATION CONCERNING THE ADMISSIONS PROCESS IT HAD DECIDED NOT TO RELEASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/X-JL
